method: Targeted repair for final-output parity: existing VF outputs keep the Source Han Sans SC VF + Inter VF merge model, and existing static TTFs keep the static Source Han Sans SC + static Inter Sarasa pass1/kanji/hangul/pass2 route. Final postprocess remaps default ASCII digits and ':' to pnum glyphs, removes old digit-colon contextual substitutions, and appends Inter-compatible colon-run calt rules for both static and VF. Static exact styles preserve upstream simple glyph flags, stored glyf bounding boxes, recursively-reached component glyph flags, composite component names, GDEF/VORG/vmtx, metadata, and hinted TrueType instruction tables/programs when outlines match. Static TTFs use post format 2 so the upstream-style component glyph names remain stable after PropDigits cmap remaps; VF keeps its existing post/GID model.
intentional_differences_from_upstream_sa: Default ASCII digits and ':' use proportional glyphs; tnum restores tabular glyphs.
Weight instances follow Source Han Sans stops: 250, 300, 350, 400, 500, 700, 900.
VF and static TTFs use Inter-compatible contextual colon-run behavior.
Static TTFs use post format 2 to preserve audit-stable glyph names after PropDigits cmap remaps; VF keeps its existing post/GID model.

ots.unhinted_warning_baseline: Unhinted fonts may report upstream maxZones/gasp sentinel warnings while returning 0.

fontbakery_sample.command_scope: PropDigits hinted Regular, unhinted Regular, and upright VF; skip-network; error-code-on ERROR.
fontbakery_sample.interpretation: Remaining items are upstream-baseline or collection-shape checks such as font_version, GDEF mark/spacing, post table version, xAvgCharWidth, underline/family, and VF axis range checks.

final_repair_verification.inter_colon_behavior: Static and VF calt were checked against Inter samples: 1:2 raises, 1:a/a:2/a:b do not, and colon runs follow Inter behavior.
final_repair_verification.hint_sync: Hinted exact styles keep synced upstream instruction tables and per-glyph programs after final postprocess.
final_repair_verification.glyf_write: Static outputs preserve OVERLAP_SIMPLE semantics and use OTS-compatible repeat encoding for repeated overlap flags instead of clearing bit 6.
